planned_changes.description: Ensure bridge_cable_triage.xlsx is saved in a standards-compliant .xlsx format that openpyxl can parse. Remove or replace any binary-only add-ins, signed native extensions, or macros that block parsing; provide a macro-free export if macros are required.
planned_changes.success_criteria: openpyxl can open the .xlsx and the artifact preview shows the workbook contents without openpyxl_unavailable error.

planned_changes.description: Add a 'Sources' worksheet listing each packet row, panel label, and the exact cell references used in calculations. For each numeric value referenced in the memo, include a reference row mapping the memo claim to packet row/cell (e.g., 'P2 AE_hits -> Packet row 3, cell C5').
planned_changes.success_criteria: Every numeric value in the memo has a corresponding mapping row in 'Sources'.

planned_changes.description: Add a 'Formulas (text)' worksheet that reproduces key formulas as text strings so verifiers can read the logic without executing the workbook. Clearly label the sensitivity-control input cells (e.g., AE_threshold_cell) and include a short comment describing how to toggle them.
planned_changes.success_criteria: Formulas are readable in the 'Formulas (text)' sheet and sensitivity inputs are clearly named and documented.

planned_changes.description: Create a 'Validation' worksheet that shows the verification outputs, the cells that generated those outputs, and a brief provenance note (timestamp, script/verifier used). If validation checks were run during the baseline attempt, include a short log or summary row showing those results.
planned_changes.success_criteria: The 'Validation' sheet documents the same checks/flags (e.g., input_panel_count, selected_panel_count, validation_checks_passed) and maps them to formula/cell locations.

planned_changes.description: Update inspection_priority_memo.md to include explicit row-level citations for each numeric claim and note any differences between the workbook and memo (if any). For example: 'P2 AE_hits = 118 (packet row 3, cell C5)'.
planned_changes.success_criteria: Memo contains a citation for each numeric statement and a short section describing how to reproduce the ranking using the workbook.

planned_changes.description: Add an alternate raw data representation: a 'raw_packet' sheet or CSV export containing the embedded packet rows so verifiers can inspect raw inputs even if formula parsing fails.
planned_changes.success_criteria: raw_packet sheet or CSV is present and matches the packet values cited in the memo and Sources sheet.

planned_changes.description: Before finalizing, run a local openpyxl-based parse test and capture a brief verification log (stdout/stderr) demonstrating the workbook opens and key sheets/cells are present; attach this log to the attempt as part of the submission metadata.
planned_changes.success_criteria: Verification log shows openpyxl successfully opened the file and read expected sheets/cell ranges.

planned_changes.description: Resubmit the updated artifacts and re-run the deterministic precheck and model verifier. If any environment-specific issues remain, include an explanatory note and attach the formulas-as-text + CSV exports to ensure reproducibility.
planned_changes.success_criteria: Verifiers can parse workbook contents or use the alternate representations to confirm claims; previous unsupported-claim issues are resolved.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Required artifacts are present and the memo documents a defensible decision (P2, P3). However, the .xlsx could not be parsed in the preview (openpyxl unavailable), preventing independent verification of live formulas and sensitivity-control behavior. The memo preview also lacks explicit row-level citations linking every reported numeric value to packet rows/cell references. Because the rubric requires verifiable live formulas and row-level citations, the submission earns partial credit and needs targeted fixes so verifiers can reproduce and confirm claims.
